Editors

Where to find video editor jobs

The best video editor opportunities are split across job boards, private communities, creator teams, agencies, referrals, and local production networks.

Search by format, not just title

Use searches like YouTube editor, podcast editor, short-form editor, assistant editor, paid social editor, and remote video editor. Many good roles never use the exact phrase video editor jobs.

Use communities carefully

Reddit, Facebook groups, Discords, creator communities, and local film groups can surface work early. Lead with helpful context and portfolio fit rather than dropping the same self-promo link everywhere.

Make referrals easier

Keep a short profile ready with your best formats, turnaround, software, rates, location or timezone, and two or three matched clips. Referrals move faster when the other person can explain your fit in one paragraph.

Join focused lists

Generic job boards are noisy. A focused editor list can help route YouTube, social, podcast, agency, brand, assistant, local, and freelance editing requests to people who already match the format.

Want better editing work?

Join the editor list and tell us what work fits you.
